prompt: Add graceful shutdown handling for the HTTP server

turns.content:
<task>
Add graceful shutdown so in-flight requests complete before the server stops.
</task>

1	 package main
2	 
3	 import (
4	 	"log"
5	 	"net/http"
6	 	"microservice/internal/api"
7	 )
8	 
9	 func main() {
10	 	mux := http.NewServeMux()
11	 	api.RegisterRoutes(mux)
12	 	log.Println("Starting server on :8080")
13	 	log.Fatal(http.ListenAndServe(":8080", mux))
14	 }

arguments.query: Go HTTP server graceful shutdown signal context example

turns.content:
1. Graceful Shutdown in Go - https://pkg.go.dev/net/http#Server.Shutdown
   srv.Shutdown(ctx) gracefully shuts down without interrupting active connections.
2. Signal handling in Go - https://pkg.go.dev/os/signal
   signal.NotifyContext(ctx, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM) for clean shutdown.

arguments.title: Go HTTP graceful shutdown pattern
arguments.content:
1. Create http.Server{Addr, Handler}
2. Start in goroutine: go srv.ListenAndServe()
3. Wait for signal: ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
4. On signal: srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx) with timeout context
5. Shutdown waits for in-flight requests to complete

turns.content: Finding recorded: Go HTTP graceful shutdown pattern

arguments.message: Graceful shutdown is implemented. On SIGINT or SIGTERM, the server stops accepting new connections and waits up to 10 seconds for in-flight requests to complete. If requests don't finish in time, it forces shutdown.

arguments.summary: Added graceful shutdown using signal.NotifyContext and srv.Shutdown with 10-second timeout. In-flight requests complete before server stops.
arguments.final_answer: Graceful shutdown implemented. Server waits for in-flight requests on SIGINT/SIGTERM with 10-second timeout. Uses signal.NotifyContext and http.Server.Shutdown.
